name: PydanticAI
what_it_does: Type-safe Python agent framework by the Pydantic team — builds LLM-powered agents with strict type validation, dependency injection, structured outputs, and first-class tool support.
use_cases: Building type-safe AI agents where structured input/output is critical
Creating agents that integrate seamlessly with FastAPI and Pydantic-based codebases
Agents with complex tool chains requiring reliable structured data flow
Testing LLM agent behavior with dependency injection (mock LLM in tests)
Multi-step agent workflows with validated intermediate results
not_for: Multi-agent role-based collaboration (use CrewAI or AutoGen for crew-style patterns)
Teams unfamiliar with Pydantic's type validation model
best_when: You're building production Python agents and need strict type safety, testability via DI, and clean integration with FastAPI/Pydantic ecosystem.
avoid_when: You need multi-language support or prefer loose typing — PydanticAI's value is specifically its type-strictness.

auth.notes: Passes through to your LLM provider. Uses standard provider auth (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Groq, etc.) via environment variables or explicit config.

pricing.free_tier_limits: MIT licensed, completely free — costs are LLM API calls only
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers for the framework
pricing.notes: Framework is free. All costs come from LLM provider API calls made by your agents.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent type errors via Pydantic validation — very clear what went wrong. LLM provider errors are surfaced with good context. One of the best error experiences in the Python agent ecosystem.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Run results are not cached by default. Dependency injection makes testing and replay straightforward. No built-in idempotency at the run level.
known_agent_gotchas: Very new framework — API surface may still change between minor versions
Tool functions must return Pydantic-serializable types or primitives
Async support is excellent but requires careful event loop management in complex apps
Logfire integration (same team) is the intended observability layer — other tracers need adapter
Multi-agent support (agent-as-tool) is supported but docs are sparse

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT licensed with clean, minimal dependency tree. Type safety reduces data injection risk. Pydantic team has strong security practices. Secrets via environment variables or dependency injection. Actively maintained by Pydantic core team.
